data.title: Meta, Netflix, CrowdStrike And More On CNBC's 'Final Trades'
data.description:
Meta shares fell on Thursday after the company was found liable in a landmark social media addiction case.

Meanwhile, Meta is also cutting several hundred jobs.

Don't forget to check out our premarket coverage here

Stephen Weiss, chief investment officer and managing partner of Short Hills Capital Partners, picked ICE U.S. Dollar Index.

Malcolm Ethridge, managing partner at Capital Area Planning Group, recommended International Business Machines Corporation (NYSE:IBM).

Joshua Brown, co-founder and CEO of Ritholtz Wealth Management, named CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:CRWD) as his final trade.

Lending support to his choice, RBC Capital analyst Matthew Hedberg, on March 17, reiterated CrowdStrike with an Outperform rating and maintained a $550 price target.

Price Action:

  • Meta shares dipped 8% to close at $547.54 on Thursday.
  • Netflix shares gained 1.5% to settle at $92.28 during the session.
  • IBM shares rose 0.1% to settle at $241.67 on Thursday.
  • CrowdStrike shares climbed 1.8% to close at $392.62.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: 'That's The Purpose of Money' – Jim Cramer Says a 'Lot of Money' Should Be 'Taken Away From' Rich People' To Fund Healthcare For Everyone
data.description:
Providing healthcare to everyone should be the most important national goal, and the government should tax the rich to achieve it, according to CNBC host Jim Cramer.

Speaking on the "Big Technology" podcast in December, Cramer said providing healthcare to everyone would be the ultimate measure of success for the U.S. All extra money and resources should go toward making sure everyone can access healthcare, he said.

"That’s the purpose of money," Cramer told host Alex Kantrowitz. "If we give everybody healthcare, then we’re a successful country. And that’s what it should be for. Tax people, tax rich people until a lot of money is taken away from them so that can help healthcare."

Don't Miss:

Healthcare is the "fulcrum" of the nation, and anyone involved in the industry knows how central it is to the country's strength, Cramer said.

Cramer's Thoughts on Taxing The Rich

This isn't the first time Cramer has backed taxing the rich. He called for a billionaire "surtax" after ProPublica reported in June 2021 that some of the world's wealthiest paid little or no taxes in some years, even as their fortunes grew.

"Why can’t we just create a billionaire surtax?" Cramer wrote in a post on X at the time. "Enough already."

He later expanded on his view on CNBC after his post sparked controversy, claiming that even Abraham Lincoln supported a graduated tax. (Lincoln signed the first income tax into law to pay for the Civil War.)

Lincoln signed the first income tax into law to fund the Civil War.

Trending: Think you're saving enough for your kids? You might be dangerously off — see why

Cramer said the government should close the big loopholes letting the ultra-wealthy avoid paying income tax and warned that the flawed system could spark public outrage.

"We have to find a way," Cramer told CNBC in 2021. "You’re a billionaire, we’re going to charge you 2%. There are not billions of billionaires. Let’s come up with a thing for this small group."

‘They Are Doing Their Best'

That doesn't mean Cramer wants to clamp down on the healthcare industry. In January 2025, he told CNBC that many people think healthcare companies take unfair advantage of the system, but they are also developing innovative drugs to address unmet medical needs.

"I know lots of people believe these drug companies prey on our nation," Cramer said. "But when I talk to these executives, they are doing their best to tackle unmet major medical needs, fatal needs."

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: American Airlines In Talks With SpaceX To Offer In-Flight Starlink Wi-Fi, Mulls Return Of Seatback Screens: Report
data.description:
The Airline is considering bringing back seatback screens on its narrow-body aircraft and is in talks with SpaceX to offer Starlink Wi-Fi, CNBC reported on Thursday, citing an anonymous source familiar with the matter.

SpaceX and American Airlines didn’t immediately respond to Benzinga‘s request for comment.

American Airlines Deal With Amazon

It’s worth noting that Starlink currently has over 10,000 satellites in low Earth orbit, which dwarfs other operators by a long shot. Meanwhile, the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) Chair, Brendon Carr, criticized Amazon for opposing SpaceX’s attempt to launch satellite-based datacenters.

“Amazon should focus on the fact that it will fall roughly 1,000 satellites short of meeting its upcoming deployment milestone,” Carr said.

American Airlines Growth

Meanwhile, Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass) has shared that the war in Iran has led to a worsening situation for the aviation industry. Jet fuel prices have surged by more than 60% since the war began.

SpaceX IPO

SpaceX is also gearing up for its IPO, with Musk possibly reserving as much as 30% of the commercial space flight giant’s initial public offering for individual investors.

Check out more of Benzinga’s Future Of Mobility coverage by following this link.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Oracle Snaps Up Huge Nashville Office Space To Fuel Growth
data.description:
Nashville Expansion Adds Capacity

The new lease will increase Oracle's Nashville capacity to about 2,000 seats across three locations. The expansion advances the company's plan to create thousands of tech jobs in the city.

Oracle is hiring across cloud engineering, software development, sales and product roles.

"New candidates as well as our existing Oracle employees are increasingly energized by what we are building here in Nashville," said Scott Twaddle, senior vice president of Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

The new office overlooks construction of Oracle's planned riverfront headquarters along the Cumberland River. It also strengthens Oracle's long-term commitment to local hiring and workforce development through partnerships with Belmont University.

Veritone Moves AI Workloads To Oracle Cloud

The collaboration aims to improve scalability, security and performance for enterprise AI deployments.

"Through this partnership with Oracle, we are strengthening our leadership in managing unstructured data and complex, multi-model AI workflows," said Ryan Steelberg, CEO of Veritone.

Oracle said the agreement will support expansion across media, the public sector and AI data services. It will also help enterprises deploy AI with greater "security, performance, and control."

ORCL 23% Below 100-Day SMA

The stock is currently trading 6.7% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 23% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a bearish trend. Shares have decreased 2.04% over the past 12 months and are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is at 38.24, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD shows a value of -2.9379, below its signal line at -2.4584, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum.

  • Key Resistance: $165.50
  • Key Support: $138.50

ORCL EPS Seen $1.82; Rev $19.09B

Oracle Corp is slated to provide its next financial update on June 10, 2026 (estimated).

  • EPS Estimate: $1.82 (Up from $1.70)
  • Revenue Estimate: $19.09 Billion (Up from $15.90 Billion)
  • Valuation: P/E of 25.6x (Indicates premium valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $249.08. Recent analyst moves include:

  • B of A Securities: Buy (Target $200.00) (Mar. 24)
  • Mizuho: Outperform (Lowers Target to $320.00) (Mar. 16)
  • Guggenheim: Buy (Maintains Target to $400.00) (Mar. 13)

IGV Holds ORCL at 7.37% Weight

Significance: Because ORCL carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

ORCL Price Action: Oracle shares were down 1.41% at $140.80 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: How To Earn $500 A Month From FactSet Research Stock Ahead Of Q2 Earnings
data.description:
FactSet Research Systems Inc. (NYSE:FDS) will release earnings for its second quarter before the opening bell on Tuesday, March 31.

Analysts expect the company to report quarterly earnings of $4.37 per share. That’s up from $4.28 per share in the year-ago period. The consensus estimate for FactSet Research' quarterly revenue is $604.8 million (it reported $570.66 million last year), according to Benzinga Pro.

Ahead of quarterly earnings, RBC Capital analyst Ashish Sabadra, on March 18, maintained FactSet Research with a Sector Perform and lowered the price target from $320 to $243.

With the recent buzz around FactSet Research, some investors may be eyeing potential gains from the company's dividends too. As of now, FactSet Research has an annual dividend yield of 2.24%, which is a quarterly dividend amount of $1.10 per share ($4.40 a year).  

So, how can investors exploit its dividend yield to pocket a regular $500 monthly?

To earn $500 per month or $6,000 annually from dividends alone, you would need an investment of approximately $268,301 or around 1,367 shares. For a more modest $100 per month or $1,200 per year, you would need $53,582 or around 273 shares.

To calculate: Divide the desired annual income ($6,000 or $1,200) by the dividend ($4.40 in this case). So, $6,000 / $4.40 = 1,367 ($500 per month), and $1,200 / $4.40 = 273 shares ($100 per month).

Note that dividend yield can change on a rolling basis, as the dividend payment and the stock price both fluctuate over time.

How that works: Compute the dividend yield by dividing the annual dividend payment by the stock’s current price.

For example, if a stock pays an annual dividend of $2 and is currently priced at $50, the dividend yield would be 4% ($2/$50). However, if the stock price increases to $60, the dividend yield drops to 3.33% ($2/$60). Conversely, if the stock price falls to $40, the dividend yield rises to 5% ($2/$40).

Similarly, changes in the dividend payment can impact the yield. If a company increases its dividend, the yield will also increase, provided the stock price stays the same. Conversely, if the dividend payment decreases, so will the yield.

FDS Price Action: Shares of FactSet Research rose 1.2% to close at $196.27 on Thursday.

Photo via Shutterstock
